action.reasoning: Observation: We have a strong baseline of test macro_f1=0.4197 (iter 11) from a CLIP ViT-L/14 checkpoint trained for 10 epochs with basic augmentation and a linear head. Analysis: There is clear room for improvement via (1) a stronger classifier head (2-layer MLP + dropout), (2) better long-tail handling via class-weighted loss (sqrt-inverse frequency), (3) stronger augmentation (RandAugment + ColorJitter + resize-and-crop), and (4) a cleaner training script with cosine LR warmup, differential learning rates, and stable AMP training. We have ~3.4 hours left, which is enough for one high-quality training run of ~12 epochs (~1.8h estimated) plus evaluation. Decision: Rewrite model.py to support configurable MLP head, rewrite train.py with the above improvements, update predict.py/verify.py to load the new architecture from checkpoint args, and launch a 12-epoch DDP training run. No eval submission this iteration because training is the bottleneck; we will evaluate the new checkpoint in the next iteration.
